bluetooth_confirm.title: Fanimation Ceiling Fan Detected
bluetooth_confirm.description:
Found: {name} ({mac})

This fan can also be controlled by its RF remote. All commands from Home Assistant will read the current fan state first, so remote changes are never overwritten.

data_description.speed_count: Pick or type the number of speeds your fan supports. AC fans are usually 3; DC fans are commonly 6 or 32. You can change this later in the integration's options.

user.title: Add Fanimation Ceiling Fan
user.description: Enter the MAC address of your fan. You can find it using a BLE scanner app — look for a device named CeilingFan.

data_description.speed_count: Pick or type the number of speeds your fan supports. AC fans are usually 3; DC fans are commonly 6 or 32. You can change this later in the integration's options.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the fan. Make sure it is powered on and within Bluetooth range.

abort.not_fanimation: This device does not appear to be a Fanimation fan (expected GATT characteristics not found).

init.description: Configure default behavior and connection settings for this fan.

defaults.name: Fan & light defaults
defaults.description: These settings control what happens when you turn on the fan or light without specifying a speed or brightness (e.g., tapping a toggle in your dashboard).

data_description.speed_count: How many discrete speeds your fan supports. AC fans are usually 3; DC fans are commonly 6 or 32. Pick a common value or type a custom number.
data_description.default_speed: The fan speed used when you turn the fan on without choosing a specific speed. Low/Medium/High scale automatically based on the speed count above.
data_description.default_brightness: The brightness used when you turn the light on without choosing a specific level. Set to 0 to use the last brightness.

connection.name: Connection & availability
connection.description: These settings control how the integration behaves when it cannot reach the fan over Bluetooth. They are independent — notifications alert you immediately, while the unavailable threshold controls when dashboard cards go grey.

data.unavailable_threshold: Mark unavailable after N poll failures

data_description.notify_on_disconnect: Send a persistent notification on the first connection failure. Automatically dismissed when the connection recovers.
data_description.unavailable_threshold: After this many consecutive poll failures, entities are marked unavailable (grey card in dashboards). Each poll is approximately 5 minutes. Set to 0 to never mark unavailable. (12 ≈ 1 hour, 288 ≈ 1 day, 2016 ≈ 1 week)
